  "findings": [
    "Power-stealing thermostat configuration causing erratic system behavior and short-cycling due to lack of a dedicated common (C) wire."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate power to the HVAC equipment.",
    "Install a dedicated C-wire from the indoor unit transformer to the thermostat, or install a manufacturer-approved common-wire adapter kit."
  ],
  "rationale": "Power-stealing thermostats often cause solid-state control boards to chatter, short-cycle, or fail prematurely. Before proceeding with sequence testing or final commissioning, a reliable 24VAC common path must be established to ensure stable control power.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer Installation Manual - Thermostat Power Requirements",
    "NEC Class 2 Control Circuits"
  ]
}
